concept_name: Potential Energy of a System of Charges — U = Σ k qᵢqⱼ/rᵢⱼ (the work to assemble them)
source_book: NCERT Class 12 Physics, Ch.2 Electrostatic Potential and Capacitance (§2.8 Potential energy of a system of charges) — table-of-contents + Indian-anchor reference only; teaching authored from first principles. HC Verma consulted for SEQUENCE (bring a second charge in from infinity toward a fixed first charge and call the work done its potential energy U = k q₁q₂/r → flip the incoming charge's sign and confront the 'energy is always positive' belief: like charges store a POSITIVE U, unlike charges a NEGATIVE U → bring a third charge in from infinity and confront the 'only the nearest pair matters' belief: the new charge feels EVERY charge already placed, adding one new pair term per existing charge → count the unique pairs and state the rule U = Σ over all pairs = N(N−1)/2 terms → re-assemble in a different order and confront the 'the answer depends on how you build it' belief: the total is identical, U is a state function, and unlike the potential V (a value AT a point) U is one number for the WHOLE system → hand the explorer a draggable q₃ + a charge slider), never copied. The atomic idea: the potential energy of a system of point charges is the total work done to assemble them from infinity, U = Σ over every unique pair of k qᵢqⱼ/rᵢⱼ, where rᵢⱼ is the separation of that pair. Each pair carries its own sign: a like pair (qᵢqⱼ > 0) stores POSITIVE energy (work done against repulsion), an unlike pair (qᵢqⱼ < 0) gives NEGATIVE energy (the field does the work). Energy belongs to the PAIR (the relationship), never to one charge. Adding a charge adds a new term with EVERY charge already present, not just the nearest. The total is path/order-independent (a state function). SCOPE: this is the system-of-charges ENERGY diamond of Ch.2. It declares electric_potential_system_of_charges (the scalar potential V at a point), coulombs_law (the pair force) and electric_potential_meaning (V = W/q) as prerequisites and does NOT re-teach them. It is the ENERGY companion to electric_potential_system_of_charges: that diamond reads a scalar V at a probe point; THIS diamond is the single configuration energy U summed over pairs. It does NOT cover the energy of a charge in an external field, continuous charge distributions, capacitor energy (½CV²), or potential energy of a dipole in a field. NEW field_3d scenario_type system_pe_assembly: charges FLY IN from infinity one at a time, pairwise energy bonds light up with their own k qᵢqⱼ/rᵢⱼ tags, a SIGNED energy meter fills (up for stored +U, down for released −U), a running pair-sum panel, a reorder-same-total reveal, and a draggable q₃ + charge slider (energy_explorer). Conceptual-only (no mode_overrides), EPIC-L-first (no epic_c_branches; misconceptions confronted inside EPIC-L via Rule 16a misconception_watch + predict→reveal).

real_world_anchor.primary: Think of building a crystal of common salt (NaCl) from a cloud of far-apart sodium and chloride ions. Bring them together one at a time. Every sodium–chloride pair that snaps close releases energy because the opposite charges attract — a negative contribution. A few like-charge neighbours cost energy — a positive contribution. The total energy locked into the finished crystal is just the running sum of what every single ion-pair adds, with its sign. Because the negative attract-pairs win overall, the packed crystal sits at a deep negative energy — and that is exactly why salt is a stable solid you have to heat hard to melt.
real_world_anchor.secondary: Squeeze three like-charged pith balls close together and tie them in place. You had to push every pair against its repulsion, so you banked positive energy into the bundle, like compressing a spring. Cut the threads and the stored energy is released as the balls fly apart — every pair you fought to assemble pays its energy back.

q3_value.name: Signed magnitude of charge q3 (S6 slider flips/scales; q1=+1, q2=-1 fixed)

formulas.pair_energy: U_ij = k*q_i*q_j/r_ij — the work to bring q_j from infinity to r_ij from q_i
formulas.system_energy: U = k*q1*q2/r12 + k*q1*q3/r13 + k*q2*q3/r23 (sum over all unique pairs)
formulas.pair_count: a system of N charges has N*(N-1)/2 unique pairs, hence that many terms
formulas.sign_rule: like pair (q_i*q_j>0) => +U stored; unlike pair (q_i*q_j<0) => -U released
formulas.state_function: U is independent of the order/path of assembly

constraints: U is the SUM over every unique pair of k*q_i*q_j/r_ij — one signed term per pair, not per charge (C1).
Each pair term carries the sign of q_i*q_j: like pair positive (energy stored), unlike pair negative (energy released) (C2).
Energy belongs to the PAIR (the relationship), never to a single charge in isolation (C3).
Adding a charge adds one new pair term with EVERY charge already present, not just the nearest (C4).
A system of N charges has N*(N-1)/2 unique pairs and that many terms (C5).
U is path/order-independent — a state function; any assembly order gives the same total (C6).
The reference is U=0 when all charges are infinitely far apart (C7).
The on-screen U uses round DEMO units (DEMO_U = 12), not SI joules (C8).

aha_moment.statement: A configuration's energy is one sum: k qᵢqⱼ/rᵢⱼ over every unique pair.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: Three charges light three bonds; the three signed pair terms −4.3, +9.2, −10.3 assemble into one total −5.4 on the meter.

STATE_1.title: Bring a charge in from infinity — that work is the pair's energy U = k q₁q₂/r

scene_composition.text: fix +q₁ — now bring +q₂ in from far away (from infinity)
pushing two like charges together takes work: U = k q₁q₂ ⁄ r
that work is banked as +4.3 — the energy of the PAIR, not one charge

tts_sentences.text_en: Fix the positive charge q₁. Now bring a second positive charge q₂ in from far away, all the way from infinity.
tts_sentences.text_hi: positive charge q₁ को fix रखिए। अब एक दूसरा positive charge q₂ बहुत दूर से, infinity से, पास लाइए।
tts_sentences.text_te: positive charge q₁ ని fix గా ఉంచండి. ఇప్పుడు ఒక రెండవ positive charge q₂ ని చాలా దూరం నుండి, infinity నుండి, దగ్గరకు తీసుకురండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Pushing two like charges together takes work against their repulsion. That work is the potential energy U, equal to k q₁ q₂ over r.
tts_sentences.text_hi: दो like charges को साथ धकेलने में उनके repulsion के खिलाफ work लगता है। वही work potential energy U है, k q₁ q₂ over r के बराबर।
tts_sentences.text_te: రెండు like charges ని కలిపి నెట్టడానికి వాటి repulsion కి వ్యతిరేకంగా work అవసరం. ఆ work యే potential energy U, k q₁ q₂ over r కి సమానం.

tts_sentences.text_en: Here that energy is banked as plus four point three. It belongs to the pair, the relationship — not to either charge alone.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यहाँ वह energy plus four point three के रूप में बैंक होती है। यह pair की है, relationship की — किसी एक charge की नहीं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇక్కడ ఆ energy plus four point three గా bank అవుతుంది. ఇది pair కి, relationship కి చెందుతుంది — ఏ ఒక్క charge కి కాదు.

STATE_2.title: Flip the sign — like charges store +U, unlike charges release −U

scene_composition.text: same move — but now the incoming charge q₂ is NEGATIVE
natural guess: energy banks positive again
opposite charges ATTRACT — the field does the work: U = −4.3 (was +4.3)

tts_sentences.text_en: Run the same move, but this time the incoming charge q₂ is negative.
tts_sentences.text_hi: वही move कीजिए, लेकिन इस बार आने वाला charge q₂ negative है।
tts_sentences.text_te: అదే move చేయండి, కానీ ఈసారి వచ్చే charge q₂ negative.

tts_sentences.text_en: The natural guess is that the energy banks positive again, just like the like-charge pair did.
tts_sentences.text_hi: natural guess यह है कि energy फिर से positive बैंक होती है, जैसे like-charge pair ने की थी।
tts_sentences.text_te: natural guess ఏమిటంటే energy మళ్ళీ positive గా bank అవుతుంది, like-charge pair చేసినట్లే.

tts_sentences.text_en: Opposite charges attract, so the field does the work for you. The potential energy is now negative, minus four point three. Sign tells you stored versus released.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Opposite charges attract करते हैं, तो field आपके लिए work करता है। potential energy अब negative है, minus four point three। sign बताता है stored बनाम released।
tts_sentences.text_te: Opposite charges attract అవుతాయి, కాబట్టి field మీ కోసం work చేస్తుంది. potential energy ఇప్పుడు negative, minus four point three. sign చెబుతుంది stored versus released.

misconception_watch.belief: Potential energy of charges is always positive; the sign of the charges does not change it.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The same assembly with a negative q₂ banks −4.3, the mirror of the +4.3 stored by the like pair — the meter drops below zero.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: U carries the sign of q_i*q_j: a like pair stores +U, an unlike pair releases −U.

STATE_3.title: Add a third charge — it pairs with EVERY charge already there

scene_composition.text: two charges set — bring +2q₃ down from infinity
q₃ feels BOTH q₁ and q₂ — two NEW pair terms: +9.2 and −10.3
every pair counts — running total now −5.4

tts_sentences.text_en: Two charges are already set. Now bring the stronger charge q₃ down from infinity.
tts_sentences.text_hi: दो charges पहले से set हैं। अब stronger charge q₃ को infinity से नीचे लाइए।
tts_sentences.text_te: రెండు charges ఇప్పటికే set అయ్యాయి. ఇప్పుడు stronger charge q₃ ని infinity నుండి కిందకు తీసుకురండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: The new charge q₃ feels both charges already there. So two new pair terms appear at once: plus nine point two with q₁, and minus ten point three with q₂.
tts_sentences.text_hi: नया charge q₃ वहाँ पहले से मौजूद दोनों charges को feel करता है। तो दो नए pair terms एक साथ आते हैं: q₁ के साथ plus nine point two, और q₂ के साथ minus ten point three।
tts_sentences.text_te: కొత్త charge q₃ అక్కడ ఇప్పటికే ఉన్న రెండు charges ని feel చేస్తుంది. కాబట్టి రెండు కొత్త pair terms ఒకేసారి వస్తాయి: q₁ తో plus nine point two, మరియు q₂ తో minus ten point three.

tts_sentences.text_en: Every pair adds its own term, not just the nearest one. The running total of all three pairs is minus five point four.
tts_sentences.text_hi: हर pair अपना term जोड़ती है, सिर्फ nearest नहीं। तीनों pairs का running total minus five point four है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ప్రతి pair తన term ని కలుపుతుంది, కేవలం nearest మాత్రమే కాదు. మూడు pairs యొక్క running total minus five point four.

misconception_watch.belief: A new charge only interacts with the nearest charge, so it adds just one energy term.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: q₃ lights a bond to BOTH q₁ and q₂ at once, adding two terms (+9.2 and −10.3), and the running total moves to −5.4.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Adding a charge adds one new pair term with every charge already present — N−1 new terms.

STATE_4.title: The rule: U = Σ over every unique pair = N(N−1)/2 terms

scene_composition.text: three charges → three unique pairs → three energy terms
natural guess: four pairs, one per charge
U = Σ over every pair = N(N−1)/2 terms;  −4.3 + 9.2 − 10.3 = −5.4

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch the bonds light up one by one. Three charges make three unique pairs, so three energy terms.
tts_sentences.text_hi: bonds को एक-एक करके जलते देखिए। तीन charges तीन unique pairs बनाते हैं, तो तीन energy terms।
tts_sentences.text_te: bonds ఒక్కొక్కటిగా వెలగడం చూడండి. మూడు charges మూడు unique pairs చేస్తాయి, కాబట్టి మూడు energy terms.

tts_sentences.text_en: The natural guess for four charges is four pairs, one for each charge.
tts_sentences.text_hi: चार charges के लिए natural guess है चार pairs, हर charge के लिए एक।
tts_sentences.text_te: నాలుగు charges కోసం natural guess నాలుగు pairs, ప్రతి charge కి ఒకటి.

tts_sentences.text_en: The rule: the total energy U is the sum over every unique pair, N times N minus one, over two. Four charges make six pairs.
tts_sentences.text_hi: नियम: कुल energy U हर unique pair का sum है, N into N minus one, over two। चार charges छह pairs बनाते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: నియమం: మొత్తం energy U ప్రతి unique pair యొక్క sum, N into N minus one, by two. నాలుగు charges ఆరు pairs చేస్తాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Here the three terms, minus four point three, plus nine point two, minus ten point three, add to minus five point four.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यहाँ तीन terms, minus four point three, plus nine point two, minus ten point three, मिलकर minus five point four होते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇక్కడ మూడు terms, minus four point three, plus nine point two, minus ten point three, కలిసి minus five point four అవుతాయి.

misconception_watch.belief: The system energy is one term k Q/r built from the total charge, like a single big charge.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Three separate bonds each carry their own k q_i q_j / r_ij term; the total is their signed sum −5.4, not one lumped term.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: U is a sum of N(N−1)/2 separate pair terms, each with its own charges and separation.

STATE_5.title: Order doesn't matter — and U is one number for the whole system

scene_composition.text: assemble in a DIFFERENT order — q₃ first this time
same final total: −5.4 — order and path never change U
U is ONE number for the whole system — unlike V, a value AT a point

tts_sentences.text_en: Let us assemble the very same charges in a different order, q₃ first this time.
tts_sentences.text_hi: उन्हीं charges को एक अलग order में assemble कीजिए, इस बार q₃ पहले।
tts_sentences.text_te: అదే charges ని ఒక వేరే order లో assemble చేద్దాం, ఈసారి q₃ మొదట.

tts_sentences.text_en: The total lands on exactly the same value, minus five point four. The order of assembly and the path never change the energy.
tts_sentences.text_hi: total बिल्कुल वही value पर आता है, minus five point four। assembly का order और path energy को कभी नहीं बदलते।
tts_sentences.text_te: total సరిగ్గా అదే value కి వస్తుంది, minus five point four. assembly యొక్క order మరియు path energy ని ఎప్పటికీ మార్చవు.

tts_sentences.text_en: And the key contrast: this energy U is one single number for the whole system. That is different from the potential V, which is a value at a chosen point.
tts_sentences.text_hi: और मुख्य contrast: यह energy U पूरे system के लिए एक single number है। यह potential V से अलग है, जो एक चुने हुए point पर एक value है।
tts_sentences.text_te: మరియు ముఖ్యమైన contrast: ఈ energy U మొత్తం system కి ఒకే single number. ఇది potential V నుండి వేరు, అది ఒక ఎంచుకున్న point వద్ద ఒక value.

STATE_6.title: You drive — drag q₃, slide its charge, watch U swing live

scene_composition.text: drag q₃ anywhere — every pair distance, and the total U, recompute live
slide q₃'s charge — flip its sign and watch U swing
the meter rises for stored +U, drops below zero for released −U

tts_sentences.text_en: Now you drive. Drag the charge q₃ anywhere, and every pair separation and the total energy U recompute live.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब आप drive कीजिए। charge q₃ को कहीं भी drag कीजिए, और हर pair की separation और कुल energy U live recompute होते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు మీరు drive చేయండి. charge q₃ ని ఎక్కడైనా drag చేయండి, మరియు ప్రతి pair separation మరియు మొత్తం energy U live గా recompute అవుతాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Use the slider to flip or scale the charge q₃, and watch the total energy swing positive or negative.
tts_sentences.text_hi: slider से charge q₃ को flip या scale कीजिए, और कुल energy को positive या negative की ओर झूलते देखिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: slider తో charge q₃ ని flip లేదా scale చేయండి, మరియు మొత్తం energy positive లేదా negative వైపు ఊగడం చూడండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: The meter rises for stored positive energy and drops below zero for released negative energy.
tts_sentences.text_hi: meter stored positive energy के लिए ऊपर उठता है और released negative energy के लिए zero से नीचे गिरता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: meter stored positive energy కోసం పైకి లేస్తుంది మరియు released negative energy కోసం zero కంటే కిందకు పడుతుంది.

assessment.mastery_definition: A student has mastered the potential energy of a system of charges when they can (1) write the total energy as a sum over every unique pair, U = Σ k qᵢqⱼ/rᵢⱼ, and treat it as the work to assemble the charges from infinity; (2) reject the 'energy is always positive' belief and assign each pair term the sign of qᵢqⱼ — like pairs store +U, unlike pairs release −U; (3) reject the 'only the nearest pair counts' belief and recognise that adding a charge creates one new pair term with every charge already present (N−1 new terms); (4) count the unique pairs of a system as N(N−1)/2 and not confuse U (one number for the whole system, summed over pairs) with the potential V (a scalar value at a point, summed per charge); (5) reason that U is path/order-independent, a state function; and (6) evaluate a system energy numerically, keeping each pair term's sign and a 1/r (not 1/r²) dependence.

questions.stem: Three point charges q₁, q₂, q₃ are fixed at separations r₁₂, r₁₃, r₂₃. What is the potential energy of the system?

options.A: U = k q₁q₂/r₁₂ + k q₁q₃/r₁₃ + k q₂q₃/r₂₃ — sum over every unique pair
options.B: U = k q₁/r₁ + k q₂/r₂ + k q₃/r₃ — add each charge's own term
options.C: U = k(q₁+q₂+q₃)/(r₁₂+r₁₃+r₂₃) — lump the charges and the distances
options.D: Only the nearest pair's k qq/r; the other pairs are too far to count

distractor_misconceptions.B: uses a per-charge k q/r sum — that is the potential V at a point, not the system energy U over pairs
distractor_misconceptions.C: lumps charges and distances instead of summing each pair's k qᵢqⱼ/rᵢⱼ separately
distractor_misconceptions.D: keeps only the nearest pair, but every unique pair contributes a term

questions.tested_idea: The energy of a system is the sum of k qᵢqⱼ/rᵢⱼ over every unique pair of charges.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Several point charges sit at fixed positions. Write the potential energy of the whole configuration in terms of the charges and their pairwise separations.

questions.stem: You bring a second charge in from infinity toward a fixed first charge. When is the stored potential energy POSITIVE?

options.A: When the charges are alike (qq > 0) — you do work against repulsion, banking +U
options.B: Always — potential energy of charges can never be negative
options.C: Only when the charges are unlike (qq < 0) — attraction stores the energy
options.D: Never — bringing charges together always releases energy

distractor_misconceptions.B: assumes energy is always positive, ignoring that unlike pairs give negative U
distractor_misconceptions.C: swaps the signs — attraction (unlike) gives negative U, not positive
distractor_misconceptions.D: assumes assembly always releases energy, but like charges require work, storing +U

questions.tested_idea: A like pair stores positive energy (work against repulsion); an unlike pair gives negative energy.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Two charges are assembled from infinity. State whether the stored energy is positive or negative for a like pair and for an unlike pair, and why.

questions.stem: A system already has two charges. You bring in a third charge from infinity. How many NEW energy terms appear?

options.A: Two — the new charge forms a pair with each of the two already present
options.B: One — it only pairs with the nearest existing charge
options.C: Three — one for each charge including itself
options.D: None — the energy is fixed once the first two charges are placed

distractor_misconceptions.B: keeps only the nearest pair, but the new charge pairs with EVERY existing charge
distractor_misconceptions.C: counts a self-pair, but a charge does not pair with itself
distractor_misconceptions.D: assumes later charges add nothing, but each new charge adds N−1 pair terms

questions.tested_idea: Adding a charge adds one new pair term with every charge already present (N−1 new terms).
questions.parallel_form_stem: A configuration of three charges already exists. A fourth charge is brought in from infinity. How many new pair-energy terms are introduced?

questions.stem: How many unique pair-energy terms does a system of FOUR point charges have?

distractor_misconceptions.B: counts one term per charge, but energy terms belong to pairs, not single charges
distractor_misconceptions.C: uses N(N−1), double-counting each pair (q_i q_j and q_j q_i are the same term)
distractor_misconceptions.D: invents two terms per charge with no basis in pair counting

questions.tested_idea: A system of N charges has N(N−1)/2 unique pairs, hence that many energy terms.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Count the number of distinct pair-energy terms in the potential energy of a system of five point charges.

questions.stem: You assemble the same three charges in two different orders. How do the two total energies compare?

options.A: They are identical — U depends only on the final configuration, not the path
options.B: They differ, because the work depends on which charge you move first
options.C: They differ by the energy of the first pair you build
options.D: Only the order that brings the strongest charge in last gives the true U

distractor_misconceptions.B: treats U as path-dependent, but electrostatic energy is a state function
distractor_misconceptions.C: imagines a leftover first-pair term, but every order sums the same set of pairs
distractor_misconceptions.D: invents a privileged order, but all orders give the identical total

questions.tested_idea: The potential energy of a configuration is path/order-independent — a state function.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Argue why the energy needed to assemble a set of charges is the same regardless of the order in which they are brought together.

questions.stem: Three pairs give energy terms (charge·charge ÷ separation, demo units, k = 1): +2 at r = 1, −5 at r = 1, and +6 at r = 2. What is the total potential energy of the system?

distractor_misconceptions.B: keeps only the largest term (+3) instead of summing all three signed terms
distractor_misconceptions.C: adds the magnitudes (2 + 5 + 6) and drops the signs and the 1/r division
distractor_misconceptions.D: uses a 1/r² falloff on the third term, but pair energy falls as 1/r

questions.tested_idea: Evaluate U = Σ k qᵢqⱼ/rᵢⱼ numerically: keep each sign and use a 1/r (not 1/r²) dependence.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Pair terms are +4 at r = 2, −3 at r = 1, and +1 at r = 1 (k = 1). Compute the total potential energy of the system.

STATE_1.label: Bring +q₂ from infinity — work stored as U = k q₁q₂/r
STATE_1.formula_overlay: U = k q₁q₂ ⁄ r = +4.3 (like pair: energy stored)

STATE_2.label: Flip q₂ negative — unlike pair releases −U
STATE_2.formula_overlay: U = k q₁q₂ ⁄ r = −4.3 (unlike pair: energy released)

STATE_3.label: Add +2q₃ — two new pair terms appear at once

STATE_4.label: The rule: U = Σ over every pair, N(N−1)/2 terms

STATE_5.label: Reorder (q₃ first) → same total; U vs V
STATE_5.formula_overlay: different order, same U = −5.4   ·   U: one number for the system

STATE_6.label: Explore — drag q₃, slide its charge, live U
STATE_6.formula_overlay: drag q₃ — U = Σ k qᵢqⱼ/rᵢⱼ (live)
